name: compendium
description: A curated toolbox of hand-picked Claude-Code skills repackaged as Cowork plugins, each credited to its upstream author and kept current automatically.

plugins.name: clarify-crit
plugins.description: Refines complex, incomplete, ambiguous, conflicted, or high-consequence requests before acting, using the CRIT method (Context, Role, Interview, Task). Resolves material uncertainty with the minimum questions and minimum visible structure, then answers directly or hands back a short portable brief. Invoke by name with 'CRIT' or 'Clarify'.

plugins.name: improve
plugins.description: Point it at a codebase and it figures out what's worth doing — bugs, perf, tech debt, what to build next — then writes plans any agent can execute. It can hand the work to a cheaper model and review the result. It never edits your code.

plugins.name: ponytail
plugins.description: Lazy senior dev mode. Forces the simplest, shortest solution that actually works: YAGNI, stdlib first, no unrequested abstractions.

plugins.name: emilkowalski-design
plugins.description: Design-engineering and animation skills from Emil Kowalski: UI polish and animation decisions, Apple-style fluid motion, a strict animation review, a codebase animation audit/planner, a find-animation-opportunities pass, and a reverse-lookup motion vocabulary.

plugins.name: superpowers-extracts
plugins.description: Five general-purpose discipline skills extracted from Jesse Vincent's superpowers and repackaged for Cowork: verification-before-completion, writing-plans, writing-skills, systematic-debugging, and brainstorming. The code/git/agent-orchestration skills from superpowers are intentionally omitted.

plugins.name: tastemaker
plugins.description: Gives an AI real design taste so generated UIs don't look AI-made: grounds palettes in real pixels (deterministic extraction), five WCAG-contrast-checked palette+type presets, and a taste profile that compounds across projects. Repackaged for Cowork (the marketing site is not vendored).

plugins.name: onebreath
plugins.description: Claude Code slash commands that constrain response length/format: /onebreath:1s (one sentence) and /onebreath:1p (one paragraph).

plugins.name: scientific-agent-skills
plugins.description: 148+ scientific & research skills from K-Dense (bioinformatics, cheminformatics, clinical research & precision medicine, medical imaging, ML, literature review, peer review, hypothesis generation, 100+ scientific databases). Repackaged for Cowork. Large plugin — disable individual skills you don't need; each skill's own license is preserved in its SKILL.md metadata.

plugins.name: claude-d3js
plugins.description: d3-viz: a d3.js playbook for building custom, interactive data visualisations (force-directed network graphs, hierarchies, geographic projections, choreographed transitions, publication-quality SVG) beyond standard chart libraries. By Chris von Csefalvay.
plugins.license: UNLICENSED (no upstream license — redistributed in good faith; removed on request; see SOURCE.md)

plugins.name: autostar
plugins.description: a* (autostar): a generalised autonomous optimisation loop — 'soft RLVR' for any artifact you can measure. Define measurable tracks, each with a verifier (deterministic / external-tool / LLM-judge / hybrid / human-gate), and it runs step→lap→round experiments with statistical confidence, mandatory reflection, a budget, persistent memory, and a live progress dashboard. By Chris von Csefalvay.

plugins.name: science-superpowers
plugins.description: 15 composable computational-science methodology skills for research agents: framing research questions, surveying prior work, designing + PRE-REGISTERING analysis, reproducible setup, executing, verifying-results-before-claiming, investigating anomalies, requesting red-team / receiving critical review, and reporting/archiving. 'Pre-registration over TDD.' A science-domain reimplementation of Superpowers, by K-Dense.
